DECLARATION OF DECLARATIONS SHANON LOFTUS IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPLETE DISOCVERY CLOSER TO TRIAL ORT REOPEN DISCOVERY (TRANSACTION ID # 100003842) FILED BY PLAINTIFF LOFTUS, SHANON

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF SHANON LOFTUS' MOTION TO COMPLETE DISCOVERY CLOSER TO TRIAL DATE OR TO REOPEN DISCOVERY AFTER NEW TRIAL DATE SET IS GRANTED. UPON THE MOTION OF ANY PARTY, ACCOMPANIED BY A DECLARATION SHOWING A REASONABLE AND GOOD FAITH ATTEMPT AT INFORMAL RESOLUTION OF THE ISSUES PRESENTED THEREIN, THE COURT MAY ORDER THAT DISCOVERY BE REOPENED AFTER A NEW TRIAL DATE HAS BEEN SET. (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 2024.050.) THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE IS A NECESSITY FOR THE PLAINTIFF TO ENGAGE IN DISCOVERY AND THE INACTION OF PRIOR COUNSEL SHOULD NOT PREJUDICE HER. PLAINTIFF, THROUGH NEW COUNSEL HAS SHOWN DILIGENCE IN PRESENTING THIS MOTION. PERMITTING DISCOVERY TO BE RE-OPENED WILL NOT INTERFERE WITH THE TRIAL CALENDAR AND NO PREJUDICE WILL BE SUFFERED BY DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT CITES NO AUTHORITY FOR THE SUPPOSITION THAT PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS PRE-MATURE BECAUSE NO TRIAL DATE HAS BEEN SET. THE COURT HAS BEEN UNABLE TO LOCATE ANY AUTHORITY INDICATING THAT IT IS AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION TO ORDER DISCOVERY RE-OPENED BASED UPON A PROSPECTIVE TRIAL DATE. IT IS ORDERED THAT DISCOVERY IS RE-OPENED AND ALL DISCOVERY DEADLINES WILL RUN FROM THE NEW TRIAL DATE WHEN IT IS SET. DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO-TEM: PETER VAN ZANDT; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2016 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Set For Trial On May-02-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Complete Discovery Closer To Trial Date Or To Reopen Discovery After New Trial Date Set; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Sep-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. O'NEIL G. DENNIS, TEL. NO. 1-415-710-5236 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The court adopted its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF SHANON LOFTUS' Motion To Complete Discovery Closer To Trial Date Or To Reopen Discovery After New Trial Date Set is GRANTED. Upon the motion of any party, accompanied by a declaration showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at informal resolution of the issues presented therein, the Court may order that discovery be reopened after a new trial date has been set. (Code of Civil Procedure 2024.050.) The Court finds that there is a necessity for the Plaintiff to engage in discovery and the inaction of prior counsel should not prejudice her. Plaintiff, through new counsel has shown diligence in presenting this motion. Permitting discovery to be re-opened will not interfere with the trial calendar and no prejudice will be suffered by Defendant. Defendant cites no authority for the supposition that Plaintiff's motion is pre-mature because no trial date has been set. The Court has been unable to locate any authority indicating that it is an abuse of discretion to order discovery re-opened based upon a prospective trial date. It is ORDERED that discovery is re-opened and all discovery deadlines will run from the new trial date when it is set. Defendants request for sanctions is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro-tem: Peter Van Zandt; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt
